violations.text: coffee creamers were observed on the table out of temp. control. The label says keep refrigerated and observed at 70 degrees at time of inspection. Per PIC coffee creamers were put out 1 hour prior to this inspection. Since creamer was out of temp. control less than 4 hours PIC was directed to place creamers in refrigerator. -Time as a public health control reviewed. Establishment is not approved to use time as a control for coffee creamers. Per PIC he will start using shelf stable containers instead. Establishment will discontinue leaving TCS coffee creamers out in room temp. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Observed establishment mop sink (located in the front of establishment) in poor repair. Faucet not working, no water coming out. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

Eggs were observed held at 49 degrees Fahrenheit on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, eggs had been out for about an hour. Eggs were relocated to a refrigeration unit to cool. Eggs must be refrigerated at all times. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Chemical (glass cleaner) was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by flies in the kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024
The handwashing sink in the service area is in poor repair. Handwashing sink must be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Milk was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit in the Coca Cola cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. The ambient air temperature was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Per person in charge refrigerator had been open to stock it. The refrigerator dropped down to 39 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Per person in charge, refrigeration services will be contacted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023

Pooled eggs and ricotta cheese were observed held at 45-46 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, eggs and ricotta cheese were placed in the refrigerator about a half hour prior to inspection. Pooled eggs and ricotta cheese were relocated to another refrigeration unit during time of inspection. The ambient air temperature was observed at 47 during time of inspection. The refrigerator was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Per person in charge, refrigeration services will be contacted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023

There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below. Establishment is cooling food in the refrigerator that is insufficient for cooling. Cooling may done in the freezer until additional equipment is purchased. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023

The floors under equipment have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023
